# omada_port_profile (Resource)

Manages a switch port profile on the Omada Controller.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "omada_port_profile" "example" {
  name              = "IoT-Access"
  native_network_id = omada_network.iot.id
  tag_network_ids   = []

  poe                    = 2 # PoE enabled
  spanning_tree_enable   = true
  loopback_detect_enable = true
  lldp_med_enable        = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the port profile.
- `native_network_id` (String) The native (untagged) network ID. Required for trunk profiles.

### Optional

- `dot1x` (Number) 802.1X setting: 0=port-based, 1=mac-based, 2=disabled.
- `lldp_med_enable` (Boolean) Enable LLDP-MED.
- `loopback_detect_enable` (Boolean) Enable loopback detection.
- `poe` (Number) PoE setting: 0=disabled, 1=enabled, 2=use profile default.
- `port_isolation_enable` (Boolean) Enable port isolation.
- `spanning_tree_enable` (Boolean) Enable Spanning Tree Protocol.
- `tag_network_ids` (List of String) List of tagged network IDs for trunk profiles.
- `topo_notify_enable` (Boolean) Enable topology change notification.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the port profile.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by port profile ID
terraform import omada_port_profile.example 696a419049039e1d13a9c510
```
